description: Publish teams-mcp-server as an npm package usable via npx or npm install -g, with CLI argument support for webhook URL and server config

userStories.title: Configure package.json for npm publishing
userStories.description: As a package maintainer, I want package.json to declare all fields required for a valid, publishable npm package so that npm publish and npx work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: name is "teams-mcp-server"
version is "1.0.0" (semver)
description clearly describes the package as an MCP server for MS Teams
author and license fields are populated (license: MIT)
engines field specifies "node": ">=18"
files array is ["dist", "README.md", "LICENSE"]
bin field maps "teams-mcp-server" to "dist/stdio-server/index.js" and "teams-mcp-server-http" to "dist/streamable-http-server/index.js"
main points to "dist/stdio-server/index.js"
repository, keywords, and homepage fields are set
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add shebangs and build script to entry point files
userStories.description: As a user running npx teams-mcp-server, I need the binary to be executable by Node.js directly so that npx can invoke it without extra configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: src/stdio-server/index.ts has #!/usr/bin/env node as its first line
src/streamable-http-server/index.ts has #!/usr/bin/env node as its first line
package.json has a prepublishOnly script that runs npm run build
After npm run build, dist/stdio-server/index.js and dist/streamable-http-server/index.js both exist
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add MIT LICENSE file
userStories.description: As a package maintainer, I want a LICENSE file in the repo so that the published npm package has a valid open source license.
acceptanceCriteria: LICENSE file exists at project root with MIT license text
Year and author are filled in
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement parseCliArgs utility in config.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single config layer that merges CLI arguments over environment variables so that both servers can use the same resolution logic without duplication.
acceptanceCriteria: A parseCliArgs(argv: string[]) function is added (in src/lib/cli-args.ts or within config.ts) that extracts --webhook-url, --port, and --host from process.argv
Supports both --key=value and --key value forms
config.ts calls parseCliArgs and merges results: CLI value wins over env var when both are present
The existing Zod validation schema validates the final merged values
No new npm dependencies added (only process.argv, no yargs or commander)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add --webhook-url CLI argument support to stdio server
userStories.description: As an MCP client operator (e.g., configuring Claude Desktop), I want to pass --webhook-url as a command-line argument so that I don't need a .env file on the target machine.
acceptanceCriteria: stdio server reads --webhook-url via the shared parseCliArgs utility
Parsed --webhook-url value takes precedence over TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL env var
If --webhook-url is provided and valid, server starts without requiring TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL in the environment
Unknown arguments are ignored and do not crash the server
Existing env-only usage still works unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add --webhook-url, --port, --host CLI arguments to HTTP server
userStories.description: As an operator running the HTTP transport, I want to pass --webhook-url, --port, and --host as CLI arguments so I can configure the server without environment variables.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP server reads --webhook-url, --port, and --host via the shared parseCliArgs utility
CLI values take precedence over their corresponding env vars (TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL, PORT, HOST)
--port value is validated as an integer between 1-65535; invalid values print an error and exit with code 1
Server prints the resolved listen address on startup reflecting CLI-overridden values
Existing env-only usage still works unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update README with npm installation and MCP client setup instructions
userStories.description: As a new user discovering the package on npm, I want clear instructions for how to use teams-mcp-server with common MCP clients so I can integrate it in under 5 minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: README has an Installation section showing npx teams-mcp-server (zero-install) and npm install -g teams-mcp-server usage
README has a Configuration section documenting TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL env var and --webhook-url CLI arg with examples
README has an MCP Client Setup section with a JSON snippet showing how to configure Claude Desktop / Cursor / Cline using the npx form
README has a Tools section listing ping and send_message_to_teams with input/output descriptions
README includes a note that dist/ is not committed — run npm run build locally or use the published npm package
Existing content is preserved or consolidated (not deleted)
Typecheck passes
